title: Async Context, Abort Signals, Deadlines, and Cleanup · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken request context, budget, cancellation, and cleanup ledger; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in request context, budget, cancellation, and cleanup ledger and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Use AsyncLocalStorage run, enterWith limits, snapshot, name, and bounded request metadata without treating context as authorization.
Compose client disconnect, service deadline, dependency timeout, and shutdown with AbortSignal.timeout and AbortSignal.any while preserving the first cause.
Allocate request time among queueing, dependencies, response, and cleanup with monotonic evidence and no negative or expanding child budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Async Local Storage Scope
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 5240 in a burst intake service near its event-loop and queue limits. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reserve cleanup time and combine client abort with one dependency budget; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AsyncLocalStorage run, enterWith limits, snapshot, name, and bounded request metadata without treating context as authorization.

Inspect the Async Local Storage Scope trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Own background promise lifetimes, observe rejection and multiple-resolution failures, fail safely, and distinguish crash recovery from request error handling.

hints: For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Unhandled Rejection Policy, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Unhandled Rejection Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Unhandled Rejection Policy, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug team handles case 2384 in a burst intake service near its event-loop and queue limits. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reserve cleanup time and combine client abort with one dependency budget; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Own background promise lifetimes, observe rejection and multiple-resolution failures, fail safely, and distinguish crash recovery from request error handling.

For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Unhandled Rejection Policy, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A global unhandledRejection listener makes ignored promises safe to continue in production.
